introText: If you work in Riverside and have been wrongfully terminated, discriminated against, harassed, or denied fair wages, The Azizi Firm is here to help. As a California employment law firm, we represent workers throughout Riverside County against employers of all sizes - from logistics and warehousing companies to healthcare providers and educational institutions.
localInfo: Riverside is one of the fastest-growing cities in California, with major employers in logistics, warehousing, healthcare, and education. The city is a key distribution hub for the Inland Empire. The EEOC Los Angeles District Office and California DLSE cover Riverside County workers. The Riverside County Superior Court Historic Courthouse hears employment law cases including wrongful termination, discrimination, harassment, and wage disputes.

sections.header: Employment Law Cases We Handle in Riverside
sections.body: Our Riverside employment attorneys handle: wrongful termination, workplace discrimination (race, gender, age, disability, sexual orientation), sexual harassment, hostile work environment, whistleblower retaliation, wage and hour violations (unpaid wages, overtime, meal/rest breaks), employee misclassification, FMLA/CFRA violations, and employment contract disputes.

sections.header: California Employment Laws That Protect Riverside Workers
sections.body: The California Fair Employment and Housing Act (FEHA) provides stronger protections than federal law. California Labor Code sections 1102.5, 98.6, and 6310 protect whistleblowers. The California Family Rights Act (CFRA) provides job-protected leave. Warehouse and logistics workers have additional protections under California AB 701 addressing warehouse production quotas.

faq.question: How long do I have to file an employment claim in California?
faq.answer: Statute of limitations vary by claim type. For FEHA discrimination claims, you must first file with the California Civil Rights Department (CRD) within three years. For wrongful termination claims, you generally have two years. For wage claims, you generally have three years. Contact us immediately to ensure your claim is timely filed.

faq.question: What compensation can I recover in an employment law case?
faq.answer: You may be entitled to lost wages (back pay and front pay), emotional distress damages, punitive damages, attorney fees, reinstatement, and other remedies. The value depends on your specific situation - call us for a free evaluation.

whyHireText: We understand California employment law and have experience litigating against large employers. Our firm's founder previously defended major insurance companies, giving us insider knowledge of how defendants approach employment cases. We leverage this experience to fight for maximum compensation for our clients.
contactUsText: If you're facing a workplace issue in Riverside, contact The Azizi Firm today for a free, confidential consultation. We serve employees throughout Riverside County and all of California.
